marketHeadlines.headline: Meta Stock Rises on Reports the Company Is Building a Cloud Business to Sell Excess AI Compute
marketHeadlines.whyItMatters: Meta up ~8% on plans to monetize its AI capex as a third-party cloud — the first hyperscaler openly selling excess GPU. Direct read-through to AWS/Azure pricing power.

marketHeadlines.headline: Bitcoin ETFs lose record $4.5B in June, eclipsing Strategy's $1.25B raise
marketHeadlines.whyItMatters: Spot BTC ETFs just printed their worst month on record — outflows ran ~3.6x Strategy's accumulation. Spot demand is thinnest since launch.

marketHeadlines.headline: Déjà short: Michael Burry takes aim at Tesla again (TSLA:NASDAQ)
marketHeadlines.whyItMatters: Burry re-registering a Tesla short after the Q1 13F — alpha signal that the valuation gap hasn't closed in his view. Watch the next 13F.

marketHeadlines.headline: CoreWeave and Nebius shares fall on Meta cloud report
marketHeadlines.whyItMatters: Neoclouds (CRWV, NBIS) sold off 5–8% as Meta re-prices as a potential supply competitor. The neocloud trade now has a hyperscaler counter-party.

institutional.blackrock: IBIT was the largest single contributor to the record $4.5B June BTC-ETF outflow — flow story is being set by BlackRock's positioning, not the long tail.
institutional.regulatoryRadar: SEC opened a public comment window on next-gen ETF structures; EU's MiCA transition period just closed with a final wave of CASP licenses; Taiwan's legislature passed the country's first crypto/stablecoin law; Massachusetts AG filed an amended Kalshi complaint over under-21 sports-betting targeting.
institutional.sovereign: Taiwan's crypto framework passage is the clearest sovereign adoption story of the week — Asia's regulatory perimeter is now fully stitched (Taiwan + Hong Kong + Singapore + Japan + Korea).
institutional.tradfi: Meta entering the cloud market is the de facto TradFi signal of the morning — threatens AWS/Azure pricing power and pressures the CRWV/NBIS neocloud cohort that funded Meta's GPU build-out.

creatorIntel.sentimentNote: BTC tape is the dominant creator narrative this morning — record ETF outflows, sub-$60K risk, and a DXY squeeze have put most crypto creators in defense mode. AI/equity tape sentiment is mixed-to-positive on Meta's cloud pivot.

aiRace.headline: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI capacity, Bloomberg News reports
aiRace.whyItMatters: First hyperscaler to publicly turn AI capex into an external cloud product — reframes Meta from capex-burden to capex-monetizer. Stock priced that in immediately (up ~8%).

aiRace.headline: Anthropic to bring back Fable 5 as US lifts export controls
aiRace.whyItMatters: Anthropic redeploying its frontier model with revised cyber-classifiers — competitive signal that the model-tier rationing is easing, and that the Fable 5 release window is back on the calendar.

aiRace.headline: Edge AI Could Become a Real TAM Expansion Story for NVIDIA
aiRace.whyItMatters: Edge inference as a fresh NVDA TAM line is the bull case that's been missing — worth flagging given NVDA's worst first half since 2022 and board-member trim signal.

aiRace.headline: Unchecked AI progress may pose catastrophic risks, UN panel warns
aiRace.whyItMatters: UN escalating catastrophic-risk language keeps regulatory pressure on the frontier labs in the public conversation — soft-tariff signal for compute-heavy model providers.

retirement.rateWatch: 10Y 4.44% (+6bp WoW), 2Y 4.14% (+7bp), 1Y 3.98% — curve steepening as front-end re-prices Fed pause, back-end catches on issuance. Fed funds effective ~4.33%.
supplement: TWS offline — portfolio state and position table not refreshed this run.
retirement.actionItem: Once TWS is back online, scan MSTR/STRK/STRF/STRD for overnight moves before the bell — Strategy's capital plan is the one crypto-equity name that has both an earnings catalyst and a death-spiral-defying thesis in play this week.

health.move: Two-minute walk between the morning brief and the open — not a hedge, just legs.
